task_list.name: Convert Olympus ScanR Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert images acquired with an Olympus ScanR microscope to an OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- An OME-Zarr Plate.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all Olympus ScanR acquisitions.
- Make sure to set the correct `Layout` for your plate. The well ID mapping depends on it — using the wrong layout will assign images to incorrect wells.

### Expected inputs

The following directory structure is expected:

```text
my_acquisition/
└── data/
    ├── metadata.ome.xml     # OME-XML metadata file (required)
    ├── image_001.tiff
    ├── image_002.tiff
    └── ...
```

`Path` can point to either the base directory (`my_acquisition/`) or directly to the `data` subdirectory (`my_acquisition/data/`).

condition_table_path.description: Optional path to a condition table CSV file.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description: Axes to use for the image data, e.g. "czyx".
axes.title: Axes

data_type.description: Data type of the image data.
data_type.title: Data Type

align_xy.description:
Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_xy.title: Align XY

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_z.title: Align Z

align_t.description:
Whether to align the positions in the T axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_t.title: Align T

wavelength_id.description:
The wavelength ID of the channel.
This field can be used in some tasks as alternative to channel_label,
e.g. for multiplexed acquisitions it can be used for applying illumination
correction based on wavelength ID instead of channel name.
wavelength_id.title: Wavelength Id

colors.description: The color associated with the channel, e.g. for visualization purposes.
colors.title: Colors

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- By Tile: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- By Tile (Using Dask): Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is usually faster
  than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- By FOV: Write data one field of view at a time. This may the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- By FOV (Using Dask): Write fields of view in parallel using Dask. This is usually
  faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- In Memory: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

tiling_mode.description:
Tiling mode to use during conversion.

- Auto: Automatically determine if Snap to Grid is possible, otherwise use Snap to
  Corners.
- Snap to Grid: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only possible if image
  positions align to a grid (potentially with overlap).
- Snap to Corners: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner positions.
- Inplace: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling. This may lead to
  artifacts if microscope stage positions are not precise.
- No Tiling: Each field of view is written as a single OME-Zarr.
tiling_mode.title: Tiling Mode

tiling_tolerance.description:
Tolerance in pixels for determining if Snap to Grid is possible.
This accounts for minor jitter in microscope stage positions when determining if
Snap to Grid tiling can be applied.
tiling_tolerance.title: Tiling Tolerance (in pixels)

ConverterOptions.title: ConverterOptions

FixedSizeChunking.description: Chunking strategy with fixed chunk sizes.
FixedSizeChunking.title: FixedSizeChunking

FovBasedChunking.description: Chunking strategy that matches the field of view.

xy_scaling.description:
Scaling factor for XY chunk size. If set to 1, chunk size matches FOV size.
If set to 0.5, chunk size is half the FOV size (smaller chunks, more files).
If set to 2, chunk size is double the FOV size (larger chunks, less files).
xy_scaling.title: XY Scaling Factor

FovBasedChunking.title: FovBasedChunking

num_levels.description: Number of resolution levels to create.
num_levels.title: Num Levels

ngff_version.description: Version of the OME-NGFF specification to target.
ngff_version.title: Ngff Version

regex.description:
Regex pattern to exclude. If the tile's base path matches this regex,
it will be excluded, otherwise it will be included.
regex.title: Regex

regex.description:
Regex pattern to include. If the tile's base path matches this regex,
it will be included, otherwise it will be excluded.
regex.title: Regex

ScanRAcquisitionModel.description: Acquisition details for the Olympus ScanR microscope data.

path.description:
Path to the acquisition directory. For scanr, this should be the base directory
of the acquisition or the "{acquisition_dir}/data" directory containing the
metadata.ome.xml file and the "data" directory with the tiff files.
path.title: Path

plate_name.description:
Optional custom name for the plate. If not provided, the name will be the
acquisition directory name.
plate_name.title: Plate Name

acquisition_id.description: Acquisition ID, used to identify the acquisition in case of multiple acquisitions.
acquisition_id.title: Acquisition Id

ScanRAcquisitionModel.title: ScanRAcquisitionModel

flip_x.description: Whether to flip the position along the X axis.
flip_x.title: Flip X

flip_y.description: Whether to flip the position along the Y axis.
flip_y.title: Flip Y

swap_xy.description: Whether to swap the positions along the X and Y axes.
swap_xy.title: Swap XY

TempJsonOptions.description: Options for temporary JSON storage during conversion.

temp_url.description: Template for the temporary JSON URL.
temp_url.title: Temp Url

TempJsonOptions.title: TempJsonOptions

wells_to_remove.description: List of well identifiers to remove. E.g., ["A1", "B2"]
wells_to_remove.title: Wells To Remove

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Directory to store the Zarr files.

acquisitions.title: Acquisitions
acquisitions.description: List of raw acquisitions to convert to OME-Zarr.

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: Overwrite mode for existing data. - "No Overwrite": Do not overwrite existing data. - "Overwrite": Remove and replace existing data. - "Extend": Extend existing data without removing it. Default is "No Overwrite".

align_xy.description:
Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_xy.title: Align XY

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_z.title: Align Z

align_t.description:
Whether to align the positions in the T axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_t.title: Align T

task_list.name: Convert Yokogawa CellVoyager Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert images acquired with a Yokogawa CellVoyager microscope to an OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- An OME-Zarr Plate.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all Yokogawa CellVoyager acquisitions.
- Unlike the CQ3K converter, this task does not support Z-image processing types (e.g., `focus`, `maximum_projection`).

### Expected inputs

The following directory structure is expected:

```text
my_acquisition/
├── MeasurementData.mlf      # Image measurement records (required)
├── MeasurementDetail.mrf    # Acquisition details and channel info (required)
├── image_001.png
└── ...
```

The image file paths are referenced inside `MeasurementData.mlf` (with `.tif` extension) and can be in subdirectories relative to the acquisition directory. The actual files may use `.png` or `.tif` extension — select the matching extension via the `image_extension` parameter.

condition_table_path.description: Optional path to a condition table CSV file.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description: Axes to use for the image data, e.g. "czyx".
axes.title: Axes

data_type.description: Data type of the image data.
data_type.title: Data Type

align_xy.description:
Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_xy.title: Align XY

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_z.title: Align Z

align_t.description:
Whether to align the positions in the T axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_t.title: Align T

CellVoyagerAcquisitionModel.description: Acquisition details for the CellVoyager microscope data.

path.description: Path to the acquisition directory. Should contain MeasurementData.mlf and MeasurementDetail.mrf files.
path.title: Path

plate_name.description: Optional custom name for the plate. If not provided, the name will be the acquisition directory name.
plate_name.title: Plate Name

acquisition_id.description: Acquisition ID, used to identify the acquisition in case of multiple acquisitions.
acquisition_id.title: Acquisition Id

image_extension.description:
File extension of the actual image files.
The metadata (.mlf) always references '.tif', but the actual files
may be '.png' or '.tif'. Select the extension matching your data.
image_extension.title: Image Extension

CellVoyagerAcquisitionModel.title: CellVoyagerAcquisitionModel

wavelength_id.description:
The wavelength ID of the channel.
This field can be used in some tasks as alternative to channel_label,
e.g. for multiplexed acquisitions it can be used for applying illumination
correction based on wavelength ID instead of channel name.
wavelength_id.title: Wavelength Id

colors.description: The color associated with the channel, e.g. for visualization purposes.
colors.title: Colors

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- By Tile: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- By Tile (Using Dask): Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is usually faster
  than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- By FOV: Write data one field of view at a time. This may the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- By FOV (Using Dask): Write fields of view in parallel using Dask. This is usually
  faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- In Memory: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

tiling_mode.description:
Tiling mode to use during conversion.

- Auto: Automatically determine if Snap to Grid is possible, otherwise use Snap to
  Corners.
- Snap to Grid: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only possible if image
  positions align to a grid (potentially with overlap).
- Snap to Corners: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner positions.
- Inplace: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling. This may lead to
  artifacts if microscope stage positions are not precise.
- No Tiling: Each field of view is written as a single OME-Zarr.
tiling_mode.title: Tiling Mode

Tolerance in pixels for determining if Snap to Grid is possible.
This accounts for minor jitter in microscope stage positions when determining if
Snap to Grid tiling can be applied.

task_list.name: Convert Yokogawa CQ3K Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert images acquired with a Yokogawa CQ3K / CellVoyager microscope to an OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- An OME-Zarr Plate.
- If the acquisition contains multiple Z-image processing types (e.g., `focus`, `maximum_projection`), a separate plate is created for each type.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all Yokogawa CQ3K acquisitions.

### Expected inputs

The following directory structure is expected:

```text
my_acquisition/
├── MeasurementData.mlf      # Image measurement records (required)
├── MeasurementDetail.mrf    # Acquisition details and channel info (required)
└── <subdirectories>/
    ├── image_001.tif
    └── ...
```

The TIFF file paths are referenced inside `MeasurementData.mlf` and can be in subdirectories relative to the acquisition directory.

condition_table_path.description: Optional path to a condition table CSV file.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description: Axes to use for the image data, e.g. "czyx".
axes.title: Axes

data_type.description: Data type of the image data.
data_type.title: Data Type

Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.

task_list.name: Convert Operetta Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert images acquired with a PerkinElmer Operetta / Opera Phenix microscope to an OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- An OME-Zarr Plate.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all PerkinElmer Operetta acquisitions.

### Expected inputs

The following directory structure is expected:

```text
my_acquisition/
└── Images/
    ├── Index.idx.xml        # Metadata file (required)
    ├── r01c01f01p01-ch1sk1fk1fl1.tiff
    ├── r01c01f01p01-ch2sk1fk1fl1.tiff
    └── ...
```

`Path` can point to either the base directory (`my_acquisition/`) or directly to the `Images` subdirectory (`my_acquisition/Images/`).

condition_table_path.description: Optional path to a condition table CSV file.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description: Axes to use for the image data, e.g. "czyx".
axes.title: Axes

data_type.description: Data type of the image data.
data_type.title: Data Type

align_xy.description:
Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_xy.title: Align XY

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_z.title: Align Z

align_t.description:
Whether to align the positions in the T axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_t.title: Align T

wavelength_id.description:
The wavelength ID of the channel.
This field can be used in some tasks as alternative to channel_label,
e.g. for multiplexed acquisitions it can be used for applying illumination
correction based on wavelength ID instead of channel name.
wavelength_id.title: Wavelength Id

colors.description: The color associated with the channel, e.g. for visualization purposes.
colors.title: Colors

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- By Tile: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- By Tile (Using Dask): Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is usually faster
  than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- By FOV: Write data one field of view at a time. This may the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- By FOV (Using Dask): Write fields of view in parallel using Dask. This is usually
  faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume more memory.
- In Memory: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

tiling_mode.description:
Tiling mode to use during conversion.

- Auto: Automatically determine if Snap to Grid is possible, otherwise use Snap to
  Corners.
- Snap to Grid: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only possible if image
  positions align to a grid (potentially with overlap).
- Snap to Corners: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner positions.
- Inplace: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling. This may lead to
  artifacts if microscope stage positions are not precise.
- No Tiling: Each field of view is written as a single OME-Zarr.
tiling_mode.title: Tiling Mode

tiling_tolerance.description:
Tolerance in pixels for determining if Snap to Grid is possible.
This accounts for minor jitter in microscope stage positions when determining if
Snap to Grid tiling can be applied.
tiling_tolerance.title: Tiling Tolerance (in pixels)

ConverterOptions.title: ConverterOptions

FixedSizeChunking.description: Chunking strategy with fixed chunk sizes.
FixedSizeChunking.title: FixedSizeChunking

FovBasedChunking.description: Chunking strategy that matches the field of view.

xy_scaling.description:
Scaling factor for XY chunk size. If set to 1, chunk size matches FOV size.
If set to 0.5, chunk size is half the FOV size (smaller chunks, more files).
If set to 2, chunk size is double the FOV size (larger chunks, less files).
xy_scaling.title: XY Scaling Factor

FovBasedChunking.title: FovBasedChunking

num_levels.description: Number of resolution levels to create.
num_levels.title: Num Levels

ngff_version.description: Version of the OME-NGFF specification to target.
ngff_version.title: Ngff Version

OperettaAcquisitionModel.description: Acquisition details for the Operetta microscope data.

path.description:
Path to the acquisition directory. For Operetta, this should be the base directory
of the acquisition or the "{acquisition_dir}/Images" directory containing the tiff
files and metadata.ome.xml file.
path.title: Path

plate_name.description:
Optional custom name for the plate. If not provided, the name will be the
acquisition directory name.
plate_name.title: Plate Name

acquisition_id.description: Acquisition ID, used to identify the acquisition in case of multiple acquisitions.
acquisition_id.title: Acquisition Id

OperettaAcquisitionModel.title: OperettaAcquisitionModel

regex.description:
Regex pattern to exclude. If the tile's base path matches this regex,
it will be excluded, otherwise it will be included.
regex.title: Regex

regex.description:
Regex pattern to include. If the tile's base path matches this regex,
it will be included, otherwise it will be excluded.
regex.title: Regex

flip_x.description: Whether to flip the position along the X axis.
flip_x.title: Flip X

flip_y.description: Whether to flip the position along the Y axis.
flip_y.title: Flip Y

swap_xy.description: Whether to swap the positions along the X and Y axes.
swap_xy.title: Swap XY

temp_url.description: Template for the temporary JSON URL.
temp_url.title: Temp Url

TempJsonOptions.title: TempJsonOptions

wells_to_remove.description: List of well identifiers to remove. E.g., ["A1", "B2"]
wells_to_remove.title: Wells To Remove

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Directory to store the Zarr files.

acquisitions.title: Acquisitions
acquisitions.description: List of raw acquisitions to convert to OME-Zarr.

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: Overwrite mode for existing data. - "No Overwrite": Do not overwrite existing data. - "Overwrite": Remove and replace existing data. - "Extend": Extend existing data without removing it. Default is "No Overwrite".

align_xy.description:
Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_xy.title: Align XY

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_z.title: Align Z

align_t.description:
Whether to align the positions in the T axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
align_t.title: Align T

task_list.name: Convert MD ImageXpress HCS.ai Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert images acquired with an MD ImageXpress HCS.ai microscope to an OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- An OME-Zarr Plate.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all MD ImageXpress acquisitions.

### Expected inputs

The following directory structure is expected:

```text
{protocol_name}_{date-time}
├── {protocol_name}.mxprotocol
├── autofocus/
└── experiment{_mode}/
    ├── {acquisition_name}.jdce
    ├── image_metadata_1.csv
    ├── timepoint0/
    └── timepoint1/
        ├── {protocol_name}_t1_C05_s0_w0_z0.tif
        ├── {protocol_name}_t1_C05_s0_w0_z1.tif
        └── ...
```

`Path` should point to the `{protocol_name}_{date-time}` folder or the `{protocol_name}.mxprotocol` file.

Whether to align the positions in the XY plane by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.

align_z.description:
Whether to align the positions in the Z axis by FOV.
This addresses minor imprecision that often occurs during image acquisition.
